segments.text: Now there cried a certain woman of the wives of the sons of the prophets unto Elisha,
Thy servant my husband is dead; and thou knowest that thy servant did fear the LORD: and the creditor is come to take unto him my two sons to be bondmen.
segments.speaker: Widow of a Prophet

segments.text: And Elisha said unto her,
What shall I do for thee? tell me, what hast thou in the house?
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And she said,
Thine handmaid hath not any thing in the house, save a pot of oil.
segments.speaker: Widow of a Prophet

segments.text: Then he said,
Go, borrow thee vessels abroad of all thy neighbours, even empty vessels; borrow not a few.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And it fell on a day, that Elisha passed to Shunem, where was a great woman; and she constrained him to eat bread. And so it was, that as oft as he passed by, he turned in thither to eat bread.

footnotes: constrained him: Heb. laid hold on him
heading: Elisha and the Shunammite Woman

segments.text: And he said,
About this season, according to the time of life, thou shalt embrace a son.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And she said,
Nay, my lord, thou man of God, do not lie unto thine handmaid.
segments.speaker: Shunammite Woman

segments.text: He went in therefore, and shut the door upon them twain, and prayed unto the LORD.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And Elisha came again to Gilgal: and there was a dearth in the land; and the sons of the prophets were sitting before him;
and he said unto his servant,
Set on the great pot, and seethe pottage for the sons of the prophets.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And there came a man from Baal-shalisha, and brought the man of God bread of the firstfruits, twenty loaves of barley, and full ears of corn in the husk thereof.
And he said,
Give unto the people, that they may eat.
segments.speaker: Man from Baal-shalisha

footnotes: the husk…: or, his scrip, or, garment
heading: Feeding of the Hundred
